jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
What We Do

For more than two decades, Reality Changers has transformed the lives of young people across Southern California by helping first-generation students achieve their dreams of higher education and become leaders in their communities. Founded in 2001 by middle school teacher Chris Yanov, the organization was built on a simple but powerful belief: a student's circumstances should never determine their future.

Today, Reality Changers serves more than 1,000 youth annually across San Diego, Orange, and Riverside Counties through three core programs: College Town (mentoring and tutoring for grades 8–11), College Apps Academy (college and scholarship guidance for 12th graders), and the Alumni Network (ongoing support through college and beyond). Through academic support, financial assistance, and leadership development, Reality Changers empowers young people to become first-generation college graduates and agents of change in their communities.

Independent evaluation confirms extraordinary impact: Reality Changers’ students achieve GPAs above 3.0, score higher on ACT and SAT tests than national averages, and persist in college at significantly higher rates than students of similar backgrounds. As former U.S. Secretary of Education Arne Duncan noted, "Reality Changers is a model—not just for the city, not just for the state, but for the nation."

LEADERSHIP & CULTURE

Reality Changers operates with a $2 million annual budget, a dedicated team of 18 staff, and a highly engaged 12-member Board of Directors. Following a recent leadership transition and years of organizational growth, the organization is well positioned for its next chapter.
Strengthened by a transformative $2 million Mackenzie Scott award, Reality Changers is building on its success, strengthening organizational capacity, and expanding its impact throughout Southern California. The next CEO will inherit a strong foundation, passionate team, and engaged Board, who are all excited to build on this incredible legacy.

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Salary: $145,000-$175,000 and up to 20% bonus
Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance; Basic Life & AD&D Insurance
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Health Savings Accounts (HSA) and Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)
PTO: Unlimited PTO with flexible, personalized vacation policy; 13 paid holidays per year; one-week paid summer break (organization-wide closure in July); 10 paid sick days per calendar year
403(b) Retirement Plan
Annual professional development stipend up to $500
Bi-annual staff outings and monthly Lunch & Learn sessions
Employee recognition programs and occasional tickets to events

LOCATION

This is primarily an in-person role based at Reality Changers’ headquarters, located at 4305 University Ave., Suite 510, San Diego, CA 92105. A hybrid work schedule is available as appropriate.

POSITION SUMMARY

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) serves as the strategic and operational leader of Reality Changers, advancing the organization's mission, financial sustainability, fundraising strategy, program impact, and culture. The CEO will provide inspirational leadership while driving operational excellence and ensuring the organization continues to deliver transformative outcomes for students and families across Southern California.

This is a rare opportunity to lead a highly respected organization at a pivotal moment in its evolution. With significant philanthropic investment and a clear opportunity for growth, the next CEO will be well positioned to strengthen long-term sustainability, deepen community partnerships, and thoughtfully expand impact.

The CEO directly oversees the Senior Vice President, Vice President of Development, Director of People Operations, and Director of High School Programs, partnering closely with this leadership team to execute strategic priorities and support organizational growth.

Year One Priorities

Relationship Building:
Establish credibility and trust with staff, students, families, community partners, funders, and Board members while gaining a deep understanding of the organization's history, culture, and impact.
Strategic Planning & Growth:
Partner with the Board and leadership team to develop a three-year strategic plan that maximizes the impact of the Mackenzie Scott investment while advancing organizational growth and sustainability.
Culture & Talent Development:
Strengthen a high-performing, mission-driven culture by developing talent, expanding organizational capacity, and enhancing systems that support exceptional outcomes.
Board Partnership & Governance Evolution:
Strengthen the Board's role as a strategic partner by enhancing governance practices, deepening engagement, and fostering shared ownership of organizational priorities.
Revenue Diversification & Donor Engagement:
Expand and diversify funding sources while strengthening donor stewardship, fundraising performance, and long-term financial sustainability.

DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

Provide visionary leadership as the organization's primary spokesperson and ambassador, building strong relationships with donors, community partners, elected officials, media, and other stakeholders to advance mission impact and organizational sustainability.
Develop new funding strategies, cultivate major donor relationships, implement comprehensive donor stewardship programs, and identify innovative revenue sources. Establish clear metrics and accountability systems to monitor fundraising performance and support informed decision-making.
Lead overall planning, budgeting, and operational processes to ensure proactive financial planning and control. Develop and manage the annual budget, monitor financial performance against benchmarks, and implement financial controls that ensure organizational sustainability and accountability.
Provide vision and oversight of all programs to ensure alignment with organizational mission, adherence to quality standards, and continuous improvement. Be a visible presence in programs, engaging regularly with students, families, and volunteers. Ensure that program design and delivery reflect best practices in youth development and equity.
Build and lead a high-performing team by recruiting talented staff, providing coaching and mentorship, clarifying roles and accountabilities, and creating a culture of excellence, collaboration, and mission focus. Implement succession planning for key roles and ensure compensation and advancement pathways that support retention.
Partner closely with the Board Chair and committees on governance, strategic planning, and board development. Foster collaborative relationships that leverage Board expertise, ensure regular communication, and strengthen Board-Staff partnership around organizational priorities.
Ensure organizational effectiveness through strong systems, processes, and performance management across finance, human resources, technology, communications, and program operations.
Lead strategic marketing and public relations initiatives that tell the Reality Changers story compellingly, showcase student impact, and increase visibility regionally and nationally. Ensure consistent, authentic communication across all channels that reinforce organizational mission and values.

BACKGROUND PROFILE

Demonstrated commitment to educational equity and experience engaging authentically with diverse communities. Ability to center student and family voices while advancing mission-driven outcomes.
Bachelor's degree required, master’s degree preferred. Conversational fluency in Spanish a plus.
Senior leadership experience with responsibility for organizational strategy, operations, and financial performance in a nonprofit or mission-driven organization.
Proven track record of developing and implementing successful fundraising strategies. Skilled at cultivating major donors, building corporate and foundation partnerships, identifying innovative revenue sources, and engaging government entities.
Strong experience partnering with nonprofit Boards on strategic planning and governance. Success leading organizations through growth and change while building consensus around a compelling vision, with a track record of strengthening Board engagement and effectiveness.
Track record of building high-performing teams and fostering a collaborative, transparent, and mission-driven culture. An engaging and accessible leader who inspires staff and develops talent.
Strong public speaking and presentation skills, with the ability to effectively communicate the organization's story, inspire action, and translate complex information for diverse audiences.
A leader who navigates change and ambiguity effectively, makes rapid yet well-informed decisions, and remains accessible and visible to their stakeholders.

jobs.title: Senior Director of Development, UCI MIND
jobs.description:
Who We Are

Founded in 1965, UC Irvine
is a member of the prestigious Association of American Universities and is ranked among the nation’s top 10 public universities by U.S. News & World Report. The campus has produced five Nobel laureates and is known for its academic achievement, premier research, innovation and anteater mascot. Led by Chancellor Howard Gillman, UC Irvine has more than 36,000 students and offers 224 degree programs. It’s located in one of the world’s safest and most economically vibrant communities and is Orange County’s second-largest employer, contributing $7 billion annually to the local economy and $8 billion statewide.

To learn more about UC Irvine, visit www.uci.edu.

The UC Irvine Institute for Memory Impairments and Neurological Disorders (UCI MIND) is internationally recognized for its research accomplishments in age-related brain disorders. UCI MIND is the University's center for aging and dementia research, with our faculty seeking to understand the causes leading to neurological disorders such as Alzheimer's disease, frontotemporal dementia, Lewy body dementia, and Huntington's disease.

For more than 40 years, UCI MIND has been at the forefront of Alzheimer's disease research. It is home to one of 35 Alzheimer's Disease Research Centers (ADRC) funded by the National Institute on Aging (NIA), a branch of the National Institutes of Health (NIH), and one of 10 California Alzheimer's Disease Centers funded by the California Department of Public Health.

UCI MIND is Orange County's only state and federally funded Alzheimer's Disease Research Center. The Institute is composed of faculty and staff from multiple Departments and Schools, ranging from basic laboratory scientists to physician investigators, with a common goal of research discovery to improve the diagnosis, treatment, prevention, and cure of age-related brain diseases, especially Alzheimer's disease.

Your Role on the Team

The Senior Director of Development (SDOD) for the Institute for Memory Impairments and Neurological Disorders (UCI MIND) is a mid-level development professional who works collaboratively with the Director of UCI MIND and University Advancement (UA) to contribute to the development program of UCI MIND and secure significant philanthropic investments to support it. The Senior Director will maintain a portfolio of donor prospects that includes the most influential, complex or highest rated donors. The primary activity of the SDOD is externally focused to cultivate and secure private philanthropic support; the SDOD’s effectiveness will be evaluated on the ability to achieve financial goals and meet metrics. The SDOD reports directly to University Advancement, with a dotted line to the UCI MIND Director. The SDOD will serve as a member of the UCI MIND leadership team and will also be a highly collaborative colleague with other development officers across the campus.

In 2025, UCI MIND received the largest donation in its history, a family gift of more than $50M. This gift launched campaign to construct a new physical home for UCI MIND on campus and established new opportunities to enhance philanthropic support. The SDOD will be the point person for UCI MIND and University Advancement in this exciting new phase.

Required

What It Takes to be Successful

Expert knowledge of all aspects of individual, foundation, corporate, and government fundraising, donor relations, and public relations concepts, principles, procedures, and techniques.
Expert written, oral and interpersonal communication skills, including political acumen, to establish and maintain good working relationships throughout the organization and with outside constituencies. Expert skills in making persuasive and compelling presentations of goals and objectives for a location in order to secure gifts.
Expert organizational, analytical and critical thinking skills. Expert project / program management skills, including skills in setting and meeting program / project goals and objectives within budget and time constraints.
Expert level skills in maintaining confidentiality.
Extensive experience in the completion of complex and detailed proposals and related materials.
Extensive experience in working independently with both Institute and institutional goals in mind.
Highly organized and able to handle multiple projects.
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office and Salesforce or a comparable donor/prospect database system.
Extensive demonstrable experience in stewardship plans leading to additional investments.
Clarity of focus while juggling complex projects and/or deadlines. Ability to meet demanding work schedule expectations.
Bachelor's degree in related area and / or equivalent experience / training
5-10 yearsFive to seven years' successful professional development and/or fundraising-related experience, preferably in higher education, OR, any equivalent combinations of education and/or experience from which comparable knowledge, skill and abilities have been achieved.

Preferred

Expert working knowledge of all aspects of the location, including current issues of concern for both the campus and / or throughout higher education / healthcare generally. Expert knowledge of the location, its vision, mission, goals, objectives, achievements and infrastructure.
Expert knowledge of applicable laws, rules, regulations, policies, etc.
CFRE is welcomed, but not required.

Special Conditions

May work long hours to meet fundraising commitments
Local, regional, and national travel is required.

Total Rewards

In addition to the salary range listed below, we offer a wealth of benefits to make working at UCI even more rewarding. These benefits may include medical insurance, sick and vacation time, retirement savings plans, and access to a number of discounts and perks. Please utilize the links listed here to learn more about our compensation practices and benefits.

The expected pay range for this recruitment is $150,000.00- $165,000.00 (Annual).

Conditions Of Employment

The University of California, Irvine (UCI) seeks to provide a safe and healthy environment for the entire UCI community. As part of this commitment, all applicants who accept an offer of employment must comply with the following conditions of employment:

Background Check and Live Scan
Employment Misconduct*
Legal Right to work in the United States
Vaccination Policies
Smoking and Tobacco Policy
Drug Free Environment
Misconduct Disclosure Requirement: As a condition of employment, the final candidate who accepts a conditional offer of employment will be required to disclose if they have been subject to any final administrative or judicial decisions within the last seven years determining that they committed any misconduct; received notice of any allegations or are currently the subject of any administrative or disciplinary proceedings involving misconduct; have left a position after receiving notice of allegations or while under investigation in an administrative or disciplinary proceeding involving misconduct; or have filed an appeal of a finding of misconduct with a previous employer.

The following additional conditions may apply, some of which are dependent upon business unit or job specific requirements.

California Child Abuse and Neglect Reporting Act
E-Verify
Pre-Placement Health Evaluation

Details of each policy may be reviewed by visiting the following page - https://hr.uci.edu/new-hire/conditions-of-employment.php

Closing Statement

The University of California is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, protected veteran status, or other protected categories covered by the UC Anti-Discrimination Policy.

We are committed to attracting and retaining a diverse workforce along with honoring unique experiences, perspectives, and identities. Together, our community strives to create and maintain working and learning environments that are inclusive, equitable, and welcoming.

UCI provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities upon request. For more information, please contact Human Resources at (949) 824-0500 or eec@uci.edu.

Consideration for Work Authorization Sponsorship

Job Title

Managing Director, Project & Development Services

Job Description Summary

The Sub Regional Leader leads operations, growth, and delivery excellence across multiple markets within a defined sub region. Serving as a connector between Regional strategy and Market execution, this leader drives business development, operational consistency, financial performance, and talent development at the sub regional level.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities

Sub‑Regional Strategy & Multi‑Market Leadership

Translate and execute regional strategy at the sub‑region level.
Ensure operational alignment and consistent delivery across all markets.
Conduct regular reviews with Market Leaders to evaluate performance, client satisfaction, and business needs.

Business Development & Market Growth

Lead BD efforts across multiple markets, driving pipeline development and revenue growth.
Support major pursuits as senior advisor, partnering with Marketing, PDS leadership, and other service lines.
Represent the sub‑region at industry events and internal forums to promote capabilities.

Operational Excellence & Risk Management

Implement standardized delivery practices, risk protocols, and assurance processes across the sub‑region.
Serve as escalation point for multi‑market project challenges, operational gaps, or client concerns.
Capture and share cross‑market insights to elevate service quality.

Financial Oversight

Manage sub‑regional forecasting, reporting, budgeting, and financial performance.
Partner with Market Leaders to ensure accuracy in financial planning and accountability.
Monitor revenue drivers, profitability targets, and resource utilization.

Talent Development & Leadership Support

Mentor, coach, and develop Market Leaders and other key talent.
Partner with HR on performance management, succession planning, and team engagement.
Foster a culture aligned with C&W’s DRIVE values.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree required.
10+ years of experience with 5+ years in leadership roles.
Experience managing multi‑market operations and client‑facing delivery teams.
Strong financial acumen and proven track record in business development.

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
WHAT WE DO

Canine Companions is the nation’s leading nonprofit provider of expertly trained service dogs, empowering children, adults, veterans, and professionals to live and work with greater independence. Since 1975, the organization has placed more than 8,500 service dog teams and built a reputation for excellence, innovation, and client outcomes. Uniquely, all dogs, training, and lifetime follow-up services are provided at no cost to clients.

With an annual operating budget of approximately $44 million and more than 340 employees nationwide, Canine Companions serves individuals with physical, cognitive, hearing, and developmental disabilities, veterans with service-related disabilities and PTSD, individuals with insulin-dependent diabetes, and professionals working in healthcare, education, and criminal justice settings. Guided by a commitment to inclusion, access, and service excellence, the organization is executing an ambitious strategic plan to expand its impact, strengthen community engagement, and reduce wait times for qualified applicants.

The Southwest Region currently serves Southern California, Arizona, Nevada, New Mexico, and Hawaii and plays a critical role in advancing the organization's mission through program delivery, community engagement, and philanthropy.

LEADERSHIP & CULTURE
The Southwest Region operates within a collaborative national structure while maintaining significant responsibility for fundraising, stakeholder engagement, and community visibility. The culture is deeply mission-driven, with staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners united by a shared commitment to transforming lives through the human-canine partnership. Collaboration, authenticity, and service are highly valued throughout the organization. Well positioned for continued growth, the region benefits from a strong foundation, longstanding community support, and meaningful opportunities to expand engagement, philanthropy, and mission impact. The Director of Philanthropy reports to the Executive Director, Southwest Region, with a dotted line to Marcia Dowds, VP of Philanthropy, a respected development professional and five-year member of the Canine Companions leadership team.

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Salary: $85,000–$91,000
Employer-paid medical, dental and vision insurance
PTO: 17 paid days off, 14 paid holidays, including an Inclusion Holiday, paid volunteer hours
Employer-matched 403(b) retirement plan
Employee Assistance Program and Wellness Programs
Professional development support
VCA Pet Perks Program (20% veterinary discount)

LOCATION

This position is based at the Canine Companions Southwest Training Center in Oceanside, CA. Regional travel is expected for donor meetings, events, and community engagement.

POSITION SUMMARY

Reporting to the Executive Director, with a dotted line to the VP of Philanthropy, the Director of Philanthropy leads major gift fundraising efforts for the Southwest Region. The Director manages a portfolio of 125+ qualified donors and prospects, with responsibility for achieving an individual fundraising goal of approximately $850,000 annually through the cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major gifts, corporate partnerships, and foundation support. Working closely with the Executive Director, philanthropy team, volunteers, and program staff, the Director will strengthen donor relationships, expand philanthropic investment, and help advance the region's overall $5.75 million annual fundraising goal.

Year One Priorities

Deepen Donor Relationships.
Build trust and engagement with current and prospective donors through intentional cultivation, stewardship, and face-to-face relationship management.
Drive Fundraising Growth.
Execute a comprehensive fundraising strategy that advances individual, corporate, and foundation giving while contributing to the region's overall revenue goals.
Build a High-Performing Portfolio.
Manage a portfolio of 125+ donors and prospects through strategic moves management, strong pipeline discipline, and data-informed decision-making.
Strengthen Internal Collaboration.
Partner closely with program, volunteer, and philanthropy colleagues to align fundraising efforts with mission impact and enhance donor engagement.

DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

Manage a portfolio of 125+ qualified donors and prospects and achieve an annual fundraising goal of $850,000 through the cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of major gifts.
Partner with the Executive Director to develop and execute the regional fundraising plan, supporting the Southwest Region's $5.75 million annual revenue goal.
Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual, corporate, and foundation donors to strengthen philanthropic investment and advance regional fundraising priorities.
Collaborate with the regional Grants Specialist to build and steward foundation relationships, support grant strategies, and strengthen funder engagement.
Develop and implement strategic cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship plans utilizing established moves management practices and donor engagement strategies.
Support the planning and execution of fundraising events, sponsorship initiatives, and Heritage Society activities to enhance donor engagement and long-term giving.
Maintain accurate donor records, activity tracking, and pipeline management within Salesforce CRM to support effective portfolio management and reporting.
Monitor fundraising performance and provide regular reporting, analysis, and forecasting to support data-informed decision-making and revenue growth.
Assist in the development and management of philanthropy budgets and fundraising forecasts.
Partner with volunteers, community leaders, and advocates to expand donor networks, strengthen community relationships, and enhance regional visibility.

BACKGROUND PROFILE

Minimum five years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience, with a proven track record of securing and stewarding major gifts of $25,000+ and achieving established revenue goals.
Experience developing and executing comprehensive fundraising strategies, including donor cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and moves management.
Exceptional relationship-building, communication, and presentation skills, with the ability to engage donors, volunteers, community partners, and other stakeholders.
Proficiency with Salesforce or comparable CRM systems and the ability to leverage data, reporting, and analytics to drive fundraising performance.
Collaborative and highly accountable work style, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, work cross-functionally, and thrive in a dynamic environment.
Personal integrity, warmth, and a mission-driven approach, with a genuine appreciation for the impact of donors, volunteers, and community partnerships.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Investment Counselor, Downtown San Francisco
jobs.description:
The Investment Counselor Principal is a strategic professional who closely follows latest trends in own field and adapts them for application within own job and the business. Recognized subject matter expert within one area. Strong commercial awareness is a necessity. Is an acknowledged authority both within and outside area of expertise. Typically a small number of people within the business that provide the same level of expertise. Excellent communication skills required in order to negotiate internally, often at a senior level. Some external communication is typically required. Provides advanced judgment and conducts in-depth quantitative or qualitative analysis to solve problems and develop new, innovative solutions. Required to think beyond existing solutions, assumptions or current knowledge of sophisticated areas. Significant impact on a function and/or the business through making decisions that determine technical approaches and strategies for the area. Citi Private Bank’s objective is to further enhance its ability to build relationships with, and service the investment needs of ultra-high net worth clients in the US.

Responsibilities:

Investment counselors joining Citi Private Bank will be distinguished and highly accomplished at establishing, growing and managing investment relationships with ultra-high net worth clients.They should be experienced in the implementation and oversight of investment solutions, and in providing advice and guidance on markets and actionable investment ideas. Appropriate candidates will possess the credibility to cultivate trusting relationships with existing ultra-high net worth clients of Citi Private Bank, and should be entrepreneurial and capable of bringing in new clients/investment relationships. Candidates for the position should be collaborative and team oriented, able to leverage a wide array of internal partners.
Leverage existing client relationships, local network and investment experience to introduce new ultra-high net worth clients to Citi Private Bank.
Build and manage a successful practice centered on deepening relationships by providing potential and existing clients with appropriate investment advice and solutions.
Combine in depth knowledge of traditional and alternative investments with wealth planning and private banking services to optimize client service and cross-marketing opportunities.
Work across currencies, asset classes, markets and jurisdictions
Engage and advise clients on brokerage activity and execute trades seamlessly.
Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.

Qualifications:

15+ years of experience
Extensive experience in providing investment advice and solutions.
CFA, MBA degree or other advanced degree a plus.
Requires advanced judgment and conducts in-depth analysis to solve problems and develop new, innovative solutions. Required to think beyond existing solutions,assumptions or current knowledge of sophisticated areas.ated areas.

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
WHAT WE DO

Canine Companions is the nation’s leading nonprofit provider of expertly trained service dogs, empowering children, adults, veterans, and professionals to live and work with greater independence. Since 1975, the organization has placed more than 8,500 service dog teams and built a reputation for excellence, innovation, and client outcomes. Uniquely, all dogs, training, and lifetime follow-up services are provided at no cost to clients.

With an annual operating budget of approximately $44 million and more than 340 employees nationwide, Canine Companions serves individuals with physical, cognitive, hearing, and developmental disabilities, veterans with service-related disabilities and PTSD, individuals with insulin-dependent diabetes, and professionals working in healthcare, education, and criminal justice settings. Guided by a commitment to inclusion, access, and service excellence, the organization is executing an ambitious strategic plan to expand its impact, strengthen community engagement, and reduce wait times for qualified applicants.

The Southwest Region currently serves Southern California, Arizona, Nevada, New Mexico, and Hawaii and plays a critical role in advancing the organization's mission through program delivery, community engagement, and philanthropy.

LEADERSHIP & CULTURE

The Southwest Region operates within a collaborative national structure while maintaining significant responsibility for fundraising, stakeholder engagement, and community visibility. The Executive Director works in close partnership with the Southwest Region's Program Director, sharing leadership of the region across fundraising and program delivery. The culture is deeply mission-driven, with staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners united by a shared commitment to transforming lives through the human-canine partnership. Collaboration, authenticity, and service are highly valued throughout the organization. Well positioned for continued growth, the region benefits from a strong foundation, longstanding community support, and meaningful opportunities to expand engagement, philanthropy, and mission impact.

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS
Salary: $138,000
Employer-paid medical, dental and vision insurance
PTO: 17 paid days off, 14 paid holidays, including an Inclusion Holiday, paid volunteer hours
Employer-matched 403(b) retirement plan
Employee Assistance Program and Wellness Programs
Professional development support
VCA Pet Perks Program (20% veterinary discount)

LOCATION

This position is based at the Canine Companions Southwest Training Center in Oceanside, CA. Approximately 25% travel throughout the region and to national headquarters is required.

POSITION SUMMARY

Reporting to the VP of Philanthropy and in collaboration with the Program Director, the Executive Director (ED) acts as the senior leader for Canine Companions' Southwest Region, providing overall leadership for fundraising, community engagement, volunteer engagement, and regional operations. As the region's primary external ambassador, the ED will cultivate and steward relationships with donors, volunteers, corporate partners, and community leaders while advancing the organization's mission and strategic priorities. The ED oversees a $5.75 million annual revenue goal and leads a team of six direct reports. Supported by a dedicated staff, engaged volunteers, loyal donors, and a nationally recognized brand, the Southwest Region is well positioned for continued growth, expanded philanthropic support, and increased mission impact.

Year One Priorities

Cultivate a Mission-Centered Culture.
Build trust, strengthen communication, and foster alignment across program and development teams, reinforcing the connection between mission delivery and philanthropy.
Strengthen the Team and Organizational Capacity.
Fill key vacancies, clarify roles and expectations, support professional growth, and create an environment where staff feel valued, connected, and empowered to succeed.
Deepen Donor and Community Relationships
. Strengthen engagement with donors, volunteers, and community partners while expanding visibility and cultivating new strategic relationships.
Advance Fundraising Growth.
Lead efforts to achieve and exceed regional fundraising goals by strengthening individual giving, expanding grant revenue, and maximizing the impact of special events. Provide clear direction, accountability, and support to ensure a cohesive and effective development strategy.
Lead the Colorado Expansion Strategy
. Guide the strategic exploration and planning process for future growth into Colorado, including market assessment, partnership development, fundraising strategy, and operational readiness.

DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

Serve as the senior leader for the Southwest Region, advancing Canine Companions' mission, vision, values, and strategic priorities while positioning the region for future expansion into Colorado.
Lead and manage staff in all regional fundraising activities, including major gifts, corporate and foundation partnerships, special events, donor stewardship, and revenue diversification to achieve and exceed the region's $5.75 million annual fundraising goal.
Develop and implement comprehensive regional fundraising plans, goals, and performance metrics in partnership with the VP of Philanthropy and national leadership.
Oversee regional operations and administration, ensuring effective management of resources, strong fiscal stewardship, and alignment with organizational policies and objectives.
Manage the regional budget, monitor financial performance, and provide accurate forecasting and reporting to support strategic decision-making.
Recruit, develop, and retain a high-performing team while fostering a culture of collaboration, accountability, inclusion, and mission excellence.
Partner closely with program leadership to ensure strong alignment between fundraising, volunteer engagement, and program delivery.
Cultivate and steward relationships with major donors, foundations, corporate partners, community leaders, and strategic stakeholders to strengthen regional visibility and philanthropic investment.
Serve as the primary spokesperson and ambassador for Canine Companions throughout the Southwest Region, building awareness and advancing community engagement.
Partner with the Southwest Regional Advisory Board and volunteer leaders to support fundraising, advocacy, and community outreach efforts.
Provide oversight and support for regional volunteer engagement activities in collaboration with national volunteer program leadership.
Lead strategic planning efforts related to regional growth opportunities, including the exploration and development of future expansion initiatives.

BACKGROUND PROFILE

Demonstrated nonprofit executive leadership experience, including oversight of operations, budgets, staff, and organizational performance.
Deep fundraising experience, with proven success securing and stewarding major gifts of $100,000+ and leading comprehensive development strategies.
Proven ability to build, develop, and retain high-performing teams while fostering a culture of trust, accountability, collaboration, and inclusion.
Experience leading organizations through growth, change, or leadership transitions while maintaining momentum and stakeholder confidence.
Strong relationship-building, public speaking, and communication skills, with the ability to effectively engage donors, volunteers, board members, community leaders, and staff at all levels.
Strategic and resilient leader with sound judgment, strong personal integrity, and the ability to balance day-to-day execution with long-term planning within a matrixed organizational environment.

jobs.company: Specialty Advertising Association of Greater New York (SAAGNY)
jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
SAAGNY Executive Director:

The Executive Director serves as the chief executive and sole employee of the Specialty Advertising Association of Greater New York (SAAGNY), reporting to and working in partnership with the President and Board of Directors. The Executive Director has overall responsibility for the strategic, operational, and financial leadership of the association and is accountable for advancing SAAGNY's Mission, Vision, Bylaws, and Policies.
The Executive Director is responsible for ensuring that SAAGNY serves the evolving needs and interests of its members while positioning the association for long-term growth and success. This leader will drive membership growth, revenue generation, member engagement, industry advocacy, and organizational effectiveness. Working collaboratively with the Board, committees, volunteers, industry suppliers, distributors, strategic partners, and allied organizations, the Executive Director will enhance the value of membership, increase sponsorship and tradeshow participation, cultivate key industry relationships, and strengthen SAAGNY's role as the leading voice and resource for the promotional products industry throughout the region.

Responsibilities:

Membership 

Maintenance and management of current membership database to ensure accuracy and completeness of member information and periodically assist with data requests.
Serve as a primary information point of contact for current and prospective members. Respond to inquiries, providing follow up as necessary from request to resolution on a day-to-day basis.
Assist with implementation of membership marketing, including marketing collateral and mass emails, retention programs, and campaigns. 
Develop and execute annual membership growth and retention strategies.
Establish measurable membership recruitment goals and regularly report results to the Board.
Build relationships with prospective members across suppliers, distributors, decorators, and service providers.
Create programs that increase engagement and attract emerging professionals entering the promotional products industry.
Monitor member satisfaction and identify new programs, services, and benefits that increase member value and retention.
Leverage data and industry trends to improve membership growth and engagement.

Financial Management

Complete budget preparation and monitoring; ensuring SAAGNY is financially viable, sustainable with the resources to meet current and projected growth.  
Banking – deposit all checks and review of bank statements.
Prepare all QBO entries – record all expenses and revenue including payment of bills.
Credit card reconciliation through various merchant accounts.
Report on all accounting to Boards including prep and monitoring of reports as required for each board meeting making necessary recommendations to Board and subsequent Committees – Investment and Budget and Finance Committee.  
Meeting with Investment Committee/Advisor and Accountant as appropriate.
Assist in every three-year audit.
Ensure that SAAGNY is in compliance with relevant state and federal laws, regulations, and with standard accounting procedures. Maintaining SAAGNY non-profit and tax-exempt status. Process annual state paperwork for SAAGNY.
Develop and execute strategies to diversify and increase revenue streams.
Establish annual revenue targets for membership, sponsorships, educational programs, and events.
Analyze financial performance and recommend proactive strategies to improve organizational sustainability.

Events, Trade Shows, and Programs

Develop and produce current trade shows including marketing, administration, payments, and logistics.
Seek, develop, and recommend new opportunities for networking and revenue producing events/programs. 
Supervise, recommend, and contribute to Promotions East Trade Show Management Team, third party vendors, and contractors.
Develop and execute a comprehensive sales strategy for Promotions East, sponsorship opportunities, exhibits, and tabletop events.
Achieve annual exhibitor, sponsorship, attendance, and revenue goals established by the Board.
Lead efforts to ensure Promotions East remains financially successful and delivers measurable value to exhibitors, attendees, sponsors, and members.
Build and maintain relationships with key industry suppliers, distributors, sponsors, and strategic partners.
Develop and execute educational webinars and seminars to the benefit of the membership. 
Negotiate all contractual relationships in relation to events, trade shows, and programs.
Manage volunteers, committees, contractors, and detailed logistics of all events, trade shows, and programs.
Registrations– emails, follow up, event set up, contract negotiation for all events, trade shows, and programs.
Develop post-event analytics and recommendations to continuously improve profitability and member engagement.

Communication 

Communicate regularly with Board of Directors. 
Prepare, design, and manage newsletter, mass emails, and social media (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram).
Update and maintain SAAGNY website including website news blog with relevant information.
Communicate effectively with a variety of stakeholders including board members, volunteer leaders, third-party partners, to ensure proper messaging, timing, and execution of the plan and support of specific events.
Speak effectively on behalf of SAAGNY in public settings when needed.
Position SAAGNY as the leading regional voice within the promotional products industry.
Promote the value and impact of membership through strategic communications and storytelling.
Represent SAAGNY at industry events and meetings.

Operations 

Manage and maintain Awards Program including design, logistics, and execution.
Develop and maintain Volunteer Responsibilities and Goals/Strategies.
Design and execute mailing of SAAGNY Annual Wall Calendar.
Maintenance of storage facility.
Establish systems, processes, and accountability measures that improve organizational efficiency and execution.
Ensure committees, volunteers, and projects remain aligned with strategic priorities and timelines.

Strategic Leadership

Lead development and implementation of a multi-year strategic plan.
Establish measurable organizational goals and key performance indicators.
Provide strategic recommendations to the Board on industry trends, membership growth opportunities, and emerging challenges.
Build partnerships that increase the association's value, visibility, and influence.
Drive innovation in programs, events, education, and member services.
Work with the Board and Committees ensuring efforts are well-organized and aligned with SAAGNY mission, goals, objectives, and policies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the SAAGNY strategic plan on an ongoing basis and recommends revisions needed to achieve SAAGNY purpose.

Board and Committee Support 

Actively participate in and provide leadership for Board including planning, facilitating, and preparing for all Board Meetings.
Provide detailed data (prior minutes, agenda, and ancillary documents) prior to meetings.  Manage and attend all Committee Meetings – including setting agenda, reviewing minutes, meeting schedule, zoom set up, calendaring. 
Maintain positive relations and communicate with regional and national associations.
Provides guidance to Board members and volunteer leaders to assist them in executing their responsibilities. 

Industry & Strategic Partnership Development

Maintain and expand relationships with key industry suppliers, distributors, decorators, buying groups, and service providers.
Actively collaborate with industry organizations including regional associations, PPAI, MPI, AMA, and other strategic partners.
Identify opportunities for joint education, networking, thought leadership, and advocacy initiatives.
Represent SAAGNY as a respected industry leader and advocate.

Professional Qualifications

Previous association management experience with financial management capabilities and fiscal accountability. 
Highly organized and definitive time management skills.
Effective contract negotiating and collaboration skills.
Excellent oral and written communication skills.
Experience contributing to the development, implementation, and execution of strategic plans, both short and long term.  
A visionary leader who can conceptualize long-term goals and translate them into a succinct, understandable, and actionable hands-on plan.
Ability to think proactively and creatively. 
Ability to manage and grow the resources of the association.
Commitment to results; 'can-do" mindset with emphasis on accountability.
Knowledge of Office Suite, Wild Apricot, QBO, and Excel desired.
Demonstrated success growing membership, sponsorships, and non-dues revenue.
Proven track record of business development, sales, and relationship management.
Experience leading profitable trade shows, conferences, or industry events.
Ability to build consensus and drive accountability among volunteer leaders and committees.
Strong project management and organizational leadership skills.
Ability to use data, metrics, and market insights to drive decision-making.
Knowledge of the promotional products industry.
Existing relationships within the supplier, distributor, or industry association community.
Experience working with nonprofit trade associations.

Compensation

$150,000 OTE

To apply lease send a cover letter and current resume to info@saagny.org. The cover letter should outline how your experience fits the requirements of the position as this will be an important factor in considering your candidacy.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development
jobs.description:
About Bcrf

Breast cancer is a complex disease with no simple solution. Research is the key to stopping it in its tracks. Founded in 1993 by Evelyn H. Lauder, the Breast Cancer Research Foundation is the largest private funder of breast cancer research in the world. By investing in the best minds in science to examine every aspect of the disease from prevention to metastasis—and fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration—BCRF is accelerating the entire field and moves us closer to the answers we urgently need to be the end of breast cancer.

About The Role

The Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development is a unique and exciting new role at BCRF designed to secure new corporate partners for the organization. The position calls for a highly experienced, energetic fundraiser and team player with a strong passion for relationship-building, an impressive record of developing and closing corporate nonprofit partnership agreements, and a commitment to generating new business. This role requires a dynamic individual who can confidently lead the BCRF corporate sales strategy from open to close, interface comfortably and effectively with senior executives and think creatively about BCRF assets and benefits to launch mutually beneficial partnerships.

Reporting to the Chief Partnerships Officer, the Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development will be responsible for creating and implementing an integrated strategy to elevate a comprehensive and sustainable corporate engagement sales program. The position would supervise two full-time current team members and grow the Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development staff to a team of at least five individuals within the next 5 years. This individual will accelerate BCRF’s annual and long-term revenue growth, marketing and brand reach, and mission goals.

This position is based in New York, New York. BCRF currently requires staff to work onsite a minimum of three days per week.

Primary Responsibilities

Develop, implement and drive a new business strategy to significantly increase corporate revenue.
Research, identify, and track a strategic and sizeable pipeline of potential corporate partners who align with our brand and values, and create a robust, ongoing prospecting and cultivation plan for engaging prospective partners in the exploration of supporting BCRF.
Lead negotiations with potential partners, ensuring mutually beneficial agreements are reached to close new partnerships.
Oversee the refinement and development of sales positioning and collateral.
Create customized, solutions-based packages to generate new revenue and meet clients' needs.
Deliver compelling presentations and pitches with passion and enthusiasm to motivate and inspire partners.
Work with Chief Partnerships Officer to cultivate Executive and Board relationships to grow partner pipeline and prospect networks.
Supervise and mentor direct reports, utilizing an empathetic and supportive leadership style to maintain a high level of performance across the department.
Demonstrate an unwavering commitment to working as a team player who supports, encourages, shares and partners with the frontline fundraisers on the corporate team.
Participate and assist in developing best practices, policy determination and management decisions of the Corporate Partnership team.
Collaborate cross-functionally with teams to share feedback from prospective parties and ongoing analysis of factors contributing to successes and challenges.

About You

Minimum of twelve years of experience in partnership fundraising and/or marketing, sales, sponsorship procurement, business development, cause marketing, and/or strategic partnerships, including complex national non-profit/for-profit partnerships.
Minimum of five years of experience managing teams.
Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing or equivalent combination of education and work experience.
Exceptional ability to build and maintain client relationships.
Ability to develop creative partnership frameworks and drive strategy while being a hands-on implementer.
Skilled at responding to evolving organizational strategies, growing teams, building infrastructure, and improving operations and efficiencies.
Bring a data-driven approach to evaluating impact and overall decision-making.
Experience functioning as a senior strategist, partnering with senior leadership team members and others, and managing a sales or development team.
Excellent communication, listening, and problem-solving skills.
Ability to build relationships and influence key stakeholders cross-functionally to manage and enhance all human resources.
Proven success leading and motivating high-performing teams.
Passion for the mission and work of BCRF.

Compensation And Benefits

This is a full-time exempt role. The salary range for this role is $190,000-$210,000. Our compensation package includes an exceptional benefits package focusing on employee physical, mental and financial wellness. More specifically we offer

Medical, dental and vision coverage from the first day of employment; with the election of certain medical plans, BCRF helps significantly defray the cost of out-of-pocket expenses with contributions to a health reimbursement account or health savings account.
The opportunity to enroll in pre-tax Commuter Transit, Flexible Spending Account or Health Savings Account and Dependent Care Account from the first day of employment.
401K, with up to a 5% match each pay period; employee contributions and any match are immediately fully vested
Paid parental leave offering 18 weeks 100% paid leave for a birthing parent and 12 weeks 100% paid leave for non-birthing parent
Paid Time Off including
15 days of annual vacation time in the first year of employment, increasing with length of service
7 days of annual paid sick time
Paid holidays along with BCRF-wide half days and summer Fridays

To Apply

Please submit a CV and cover letter expressing your intention to apply for the role. After uploading your CV, please select the "upload attachment" option on the next screen to provide your cover letter.

Only candidates short-listed for an initial interview will be contacted. Please, no phone calls or emails.

Our Commitments

BCRF upholds an equal opportunity employment policy and employs personnel without regard to race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, ancestry, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, age, physical or mental ability, veteran status, military obligations, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law. This policy applies to all applicants, employees, consultants, third-party workers.

Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full‑time basis without the need for employer support or sponsorship now or in the future.

jobs.title: Assistant Program Director
jobs.description:
Overview

Assistant Program Director

Non-Profit Leaders Can Work Anywhere
…. The
BEST Work with Us!

Salary: $82,750

A nationally recognized comprehensive Health and Human Services Agency, with over 60 programs across New York City and greater New York Area.

Samaritan Daytop Village serves over 33,000 New Yorkers annually within your neighborhoods and communities, so our success depends on those we employ.

The Role

Under general direction of the Program Director, the Assistant Program Director will provide administrative and clinical supervision as needed, if applicable to multidisciplinary staff assigned to case management, housing, and/or community benefits team. In addition, this incumbent is responsible for managing and monitoring day-to-day operations, clinical service delivery and participates in activities to monitor the quality and appropriateness of program services. The Assistant Director will further coordinate appropriate service coverage for the facility, provides on-call administration as needed and manages facility or clinical emergencies. This work is carried out in support of the mission and goals of Samaritan Daytop Village.

Responsibilities

What You Will Do

Supervises and monitors Shelter environment to ensure that behavioral guidelines are followed, a therapeutic milieu is maintained, and interpersonal relationships remain positive.
Coordinates and implements clinical services for multidisciplinary case management team.
Helps implement and monitor policies, procedures and systems necessary for effective and efficient delivery of program services.
Provides regularly scheduled clinical and administrative supervision to assigned staff.
Documents supervisory sessions with staff, completes appraisals and administers discipline as required.
Makes recommendations on hiring and terminating of staff.
Helps identify staff’s training needs and, in response, plans, develops and/or conducts training towards maintaining and improving clinical staff competency and compliance with agency policy and procedure.
Ensures maintenance of accurate, complete and timely client treatment records by staff that complies with regulatory requirements and agency internal policy and procedure.

Qualifications

Who You Will Be

Associates Degree with 15+ years of progressive experience working with homeless population, 10+ years of experience supervising social service/human services staff in shelter/transitional or permanent supportive housing and experience working with the NYC Department of Homeless Services (DHS) or other similar city operated organization.
Strong leadership and effective management skills.
Ability to implement and monitor policy and procedure in accordance with agency guidelines.
Knowledgeable about Federal, State and Local law and regulation governing substance abuse treatment programs.
Good analytical, organizational skills and problem-solving skills

About SCEH

The Society for Clinical and Experimental Hypnosis is a nonprofit dedicated to advancing excellence and progress in hypnosis research, education, and clinical practice.

About The Role

The Society for Clinical and Experimental Hypnosis (SCEH) is seeking a self-starting Executive Director to lead the day-to-day management of the Society as it enters its next chapter. This is the organization’s sole staff role -- you will effectively serve as its headquarters office, working closely with an elected President and a small Executive Committee of psychologists, physicians, and researchers to keep the Society running smoothly and its programs thriving. We’re looking for someone strategic, collaborative, and service-oriented, with the interpersonal skills and organizational discipline to manage many priorities at once.

Responsibilities

Manage the Society’s operations.
Partner with the President and Executive Committee to lead marketing, operations, finances, and events.
Serve as the primary point of contact for members, prospective members, committees, program participants, vendors, and business partners.
Plan and produce the Society’s flagship events: a 5-day annual conference each fall - held in person in the U.S. every other year - and a 4-day online spring workshop.
Carry out bookkeeping and day-to-day financial management.
Support governance and coordination by attending monthly Executive Committee meetings, a weekly meeting with the President, and committee meetings as needed.

Qualifications

Demonstrated association management experience and a solid understanding of nonprofit organizational management, operations, and governance.
Strong strategic, communication, and technical skills, with excellent attention to detail.
Experience working with volunteers.
A proven ability to juggle multiple priorities and deadlines independently.
Proficiency with QuickBooks, association management systems (AMS), Microsoft Office, and Zoom.

At a Glance

Hours:
~25 hours per week (part-time)

Type:
Independent contractor

Location
: Remote within the U.S.; Massachusetts or New York strongly preferred

Travel:
Required for the in-person Annual Conference (in the U.S. every 2-3 years)

How To Apply

Please email the following items to ExecutiveDirector@sceh.us:

A short cover letter (including your available start date) highlighting your suitability for this position
Your resume

Application deadline: August 20, 2026

SCEH is an equal-opportunity organization and welcomes applications from candidates of all backgrounds.

jobs.title: VP, Senior Program Director
jobs.description:
Senior Program Director

As a senior leader at Nasdaq, you will be accountable for shaping and delivering large-scale, enterprise transformation programs that advance our position as a global technology company powering markets and economies. This role requires the ability to translate complex business strategies into execution across Financial Market Infrastructures (FMIs) and Tier 1 banking clients.

You’ll thrive in this position if you bring executive presence, deep capital markets expertise, and the ability to mobilize global teams to deliver measurable impact across complex, highly regulated environments.

Key Responsibilities

Define and lead strategy and execution for large-scale, multi-year transformation programs, ensuring alignment with client business priorities and Nasdaq’s global objectives.
Represent Nasdaq with senior client stakeholders, including C-suite leaders and steering committees, building trusted relationships and influencing key decisions.
Establish and maintain strong program governance frameworks, ensuring effective oversight of risk, financials, delivery, and reporting.
Drive operational excellence across program delivery, including scope, schedule, budget (20M+ annually), and quality management.
Lead complex transformation initiatives spanning technology modernization, operating model redesign, and regulatory-driven change.
Build and inspire high-performing, cross-functional teams across geographies, ensuring effective resource planning and delivery discipline.

Required Qualifications

Extensive (20+ years) leadership experience in program management and transformation delivery within capital markets, banking, or financial market infrastructures.
Proven success leading large-scale, complex programs across Tier 1 banks or FMIs (e.g., exchanges, clearing houses, custodians, central banks).
Strong executive presence with demonstrated ability to engage and influence C-suite stakeholders and build long-term partnerships.
Deep understanding of front-to-back capital markets operations, including trading, clearing, custody, and post-trade processing.
Demonstrated experience managing large program financials, including budget oversight, forecasting, and commercial governance.
Exceptional communication, governance, and stakeholder management capabilities in global, regulated environments.
Must be fluent in Spanish to communicate with clients.

Preferred Qualifications

Advanced degree (e.g., MBA) or equivalent senior leadership training.
Experience delivering enterprise-wide technology implementations and data transformation initiatives.
Relevant certifications (e.g., PMP, PgMP, PRINCE2, MSP, Agile, SAFe).

This position will be located in New York City, NY and offers the opportunity for a hybrid work environment at least 3 days a week in-office, subject to change, providing flexibility and accessibility for qualified candidates.

Come as You Are

Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and identities.

We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives, experiences, and identities are valued and celebrated.

We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodation throughout the hiring process.

What We Offer

We’re proud to offer a competitive rewards package that is meaningful, recognizes the unique needs of our employees and their families and incentivizes employees for their contribution to Nasdaq’s overall success.

The base pay range for this role is $178,000 - $267,000. In addition to base salary, Nasdaq provides a generous annual bonus/commission (short-term incentive), and equity (long-term incentive), comprehensive benefits, and opportunity for growth. Exact compensation may vary based on several job-related factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to: skill set, experience, education/training, business needs and market demands.

Nasdaq’s programs and rewards are intended to allow our employees to:

Secure Wealth: 401(k) program with 6% employer match, Employee Stock Purchase Program with 15% discount, Student loan repayment program up to $10k, Company paid life and disability plans, Generous paid time off
Prioritize Health: Comprehensive medical, dental and vision coverage, Health spending account with employer contribution, Paid flex days to support mental wellbeing, Gym membership discounts
Care for Family: Hybrid home/office schedule (for most positions), Paid parental leave, Fertility benefits, Paid bereavement leave
Connect with Community: Company gift matching program, Employee resource groups, Paid volunteer days
Grow Career: Education Assistance Program, Robust job skills training and Professional development opportunities

For more information, visit
Nasdaq Benefits & Rewards Career page .

jobs.title: Managing Director, Head of Development (Connecticut) - Hybrid
jobs.description:
ROLE TITLE:
Managing Director, Head of Development (Connecticut)

POSITION REPORTS TO:
Vice President, Field Fundraising

This role may be eligible for a monetary recruitment incentive.

LOCATION:
Must be based in the Connecticut area, with the ability to travel to be in person regularly in Hartford, New Haven, and Fairfield Counties to meet with donors, and the ability to be in person at TFA’s New Haven office one day per week (Mondays) for Connecticut regional team meetings.

What You’ll Do

All prospect & donor management roles on Field Fundraising teams are responsible for cultivating, stewarding, and managing a portfolio of donors in our local communities who share our commitment to ensuring that all children have access to an equitable and excellent education. As the lead fundraiser for TFA’s Connecticut Region, you will be a part of a team-based effort charged with developing and executing on a long-term vision and strategy to achieve our development goals for the region, and managing a private fundraising portfolio. In your role, you will partner with the Connecticut Executive Director, regional advisory board members, and other members of the Field Fundraising team to maximize giving, playing a key role in new donor strategy and acquisition over the next 3-5 years.

Reporting to the VP, Field Fundraising, you will be charged with setting the vision and strategies to raise $2.6 million for TFA Connecticut in fiscal year 2027. You will work directly with the Executive Director to cultivate and steward your portfolio of private donors, and in some circumstances, you may also be the primary solicitor. We are looking for a specialist in fundraising and development who - in partnership with the Executive Director and the Vice President, Field Fundraising - can independently develop and execute the strategy to acquire, cultivate, retain, and diversify your portfolio of private donors. In service of this work, you will have access to supports and resources on the Field Fundraising team, including colleagues specializing in fundraising events, marketing & communications, operations, and prospect research. Additionally, you will partner with a dedicated Director of Grant Writing, who is responsible for all grant writing deliverables, such as proposals, reports, and letters of inquiry.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Private Donor Portfolio Management (40%)

Directly grow, cultivate, and steward a portfolio of private donors with a focus on relationship depth and revenue generation.
Deepen trust-based relationships with prospects and donors that drive increased giving overtime.
Support and activate the ED in advancing top-tier donor relationships and closing major gifts.

Executive Partnership & Advisory Board Leadership (25%)

Serve as a trusted advisor and true thought leader to the Executive Director, helping drive fundraising strategy, execution, and board engagement.
Manage up effectively - to align on strategy and ensure the ED is prepared, and positioned for high impact engagements.
Support management of the Connecticut Advisory Board, including meeting preparation, member cultivation, and enabling board members in their fundraising efforts.

Portfolio Strategy & Revenue Leadership (30%)

Set and drive a clear fundraising strategy in close collaboration with the Executive Director and VPFF to maximize revenue and portfolio performance.
Translate regional priorities into a focused donor strategy & plan.
Proactively guide priorities, bringing recommendations and clarity to the ED.
Identify opportunities to strengthen pipeline, engagement, and overall portfolio health.
Effectively collaborate with regional and development colleagues to execute the fundraising plan, and maximize local revenue in Connecticut and for the enterprise at large.

Data & Portfolio Management (5%)

Maintain accurate record in Salesforce
Leverage systems and data to track progress, inform strategy, and adjust approach.

Your Experience

Your areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role (minimum qualifications):

Relationship Building and Management
Highly skilled relationship builder, particularly with external prospects and donors
Build and execute comprehensive relationship management plans across a portfolio of donors
Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Fundraising and Development
Specialize in best practices of development and fundraising
Track record of successfully navigating complex philanthropic landscapes to reach revenue goals
Experience stewarding a diverse portfolio of donors from various private funding streams
Leverage historical local development trends in order to set strategy and/or adjust course as necessary

Portfolio Management
Set vision and direction for a specific portfolio of local donors and independently execute on that strategy in service of maximizing revenue to Teach For America as an enterprise
Monitor progress against the portfolio to ensure continuous donor stewardship

Prior Experience

Required: At least 7+ years of related experience in development and fundraising context
Required: Track record of meeting and exceeding ambitious goals as a donor portfolio manager
Required: Bachelor’s Degree
Preferred: Fundraising certification (i.e. CRFE/CRFM or the equivalent) and/or related Master’s Degree (e.g. MBA, M, Non-Profit Management)
Preferred: Experience cultivating and expanding new fundraising markets and revenue
Preferred: Knowledge and experience in the Connecticut fundraising landscape

Work Demands

Occasional weekend or evening work hours required.
Must be able to travel to engage with local donors, and travel for in person staff/other required regional meetings

YOUR FUTURE TEAM

The Revenue and Development team at Teach For America inspires donors and champions to contribute to shaping the future of our country by investing in Teach For America’s work with students nationally and regionally. We aim to source investments that fuel our work and impact with a revenue growth trajectory to raise $300 million annually. This role will be an essential part of the Field Fundraising arm of the Revenue and Development team, maximizing the contributions of local donors across multiple regions of the country.

The Managing Director (MD) reports directly to the Vice President, Field Fundraising responsible for leading field fundraising - talent, strategy, and outcomes - across a portfolio of 8 TFA regions. The MD serves as the Connecticut Executive Director’s on-the-ground fundraising leader, and is a critical partner to the Connecticut Regional Team, operating with deep integration with regional programmatic strategy and external positioning.

Your Compensation

The applicable salary range for each U.S.-based role is based on where the employee works and is aligned to one of 3 tiers according to a cost of labor index in that geographic area. Starting pay for the successful applicant will depend on a variety of job-related factors, which may include education, training, experience, location, business needs, or market demands. New hires are typically brought into the organization at a salary between the range minimum and the salary range midpoint depending on qualifications, internal equity, and the budgeted amount for the role.
The expected salary ranges for this role are set forth below. These ranges may be modified in the future.

Tier A: $90,000 – $122,800

Tier B: $98,000 – $133,800

Tier C : $106,100 – $144,800

You can view which tier applies to where you plan to work here.

jobs.title: Assistant Program Director
jobs.description:
Overview

Assistant Program Director

Salary: $81,217

A nationally recognized comprehensive Health and Human Services Agency, with over 60 programs across New York City and greater New York Area.

Samaritan Daytop Village serves over 33,000 New Yorkers annually within your neighborhoods and communities, so our success depends on those we employ.

The Role

Under general direction of the Program Director, the Assistant Program Director will provide administrative and clinical supervision as needed, if applicable to multidisciplinary staff assigned to case management, housing, and/or community benefits team. In addition, this incumbent is responsible for managing and monitoring day-to-day operations, clinical service delivery and participates in activities to monitor the quality and appropriateness of program services. The Assistant Director will further coordinate appropriate service coverage for the facility, provides on-call administration as needed and manages facility or clinical emergencies. This work is carried out in support of the mission and goals of Samaritan Daytop Village.

Responsibilities

What You Will Do

Supervises and monitors Shelter environment to ensure that behavioral guidelines are followed, a therapeutic milieu is maintained, and interpersonal relationships remain positive.
Coordinates and implements clinical services for multidisciplinary case management team.
Helps implement and monitor policies, procedures and systems necessary for effective and efficient delivery of program services.
Provides regularly scheduled clinical and administrative supervision to assigned staff.
Documents supervisory sessions with staff, completes appraisals and administers discipline as required.
Makes recommendations on hiring and terminating of staff.
Helps identify staff’s training needs and, in response, plans, develops and/or conducts training towards maintaining and improving clinical staff competency and compliance with agency policy and procedure.
Ensures maintenance of accurate, complete and timely client treatment records by staff that complies with regulatory requirements and agency internal policy and procedure.

Qualifications

Who You Will Be

Associates Degree with 15+ years of progressive experience working with homeless population, 10+ years of experience supervising social service/human services staff in shelter/transitional or permanent supportive housing and experience working with the NYC Department of Homeless Services (DHS) or other similar city operated organization.
Strong leadership and effective management skills.
Ability to implement and monitor policy and procedure in accordance with agency guidelines.
Knowledgeable about Federal, State and Local law and regulation governing substance abuse treatment programs.
Good analytical, organizational skills and problem-solving skills

The Managing Director of Capital Markets collaborates with Commercial Bank segment groups across the enterprise to identify and convert securities opportunities.
