new_jobs.title: Senior Director of Development, Herbert Wertheim College of Business
new_jobs.description:
Department

FSU Division of University Advancement

Florida State University is currently in the leadership phase of a comprehensive campaign. As the leadership phase of the campaign begins, the Division of University Advancement is accelerating the hiring of additional development officers’ campus-wide and is excited to build out its team. For Advancement professionals who are interested in being a part of a comprehensive campaign from the beginning for their career development, the timing is ideal to join our team.

Responsibilities

The Senior Director of Development strategically designs and executes a personal solicitation program resulting in philanthropic support through the identification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of high-level prospects for the Herbert Wertheim College of Business. The Senior Director of Development secures, on an annual basis, private support at the major gift level ($100,000) while also identifying, developing and managing a portfolio primarily consisting of $1,000,000 and above donors. The Senior Director of Development will be expected to uphold and exemplify the operating practices of the Division of University Advancement while raising philanthropic dollars in support of the mission of Florida State University.

Strategic Fundraising Development

Serving as the chief strategist, develops, oversees and coordinates the fundraising plans of the College with the Dean and the Associate Vice President, Colleges, Schools and Units. Identifies, qualifies and sustains a donor prospect pipeline and portfolio with a giving capacity of $100,000 or more, while concentrating on the development of major and principal gift prospects at the $1,000,000 and up level. Provides oversight and assistance in the determination of the feasibility and type of giving vehicle best suited to the needs of the donor, the Herbert Wertheim College of Business and the University. Prepares prospect materials for the President of the University, the Vice President, the Associate Vice President, the Dean and other senior administration as appropriate to a prospect’s situation.

Effectively communicates FSU’s mission and represents the FSU’s culture to external stakeholders.

Travels frequently to meet with alumni, parents, faculty, staff and friends of FSU.

Management

Supervises and assists in the professional development and mentoring of the Herbert Wertheim College of Business fundraising staff, to include the monitoring of fundraising metrics. Leads staff in establishing a strong philanthropic focused and supportive work environment in alignment with the FSU’s operating principles. Facilitates the development, maintenance and monitoring of strategically aligned staff work plans. Responsible for cost center budget development and management, including expense reports under the direction of the Associate Vice President of Advancement, Colleges, Schools and Units.

Administrative/Events

Facilitates meetings, initiating up to date communications, informational exchanges and project / philanthropic coordination with the faculty and faculty chairs. Prepares correspondence with prospects and all contact reporting in a professional and timely manner. Maintains donor interest and enthusiasm through stewardship activities. Prepares proposals and gift agreements as required for proper gift documentation. Reviews gift agreements for proper gift documentation.

Board/Volunteer Management

Designs and develops a volunteer structure in support of the Herbert Wertheim College of Business’ fundraising efforts. Ensures direct activities with prospects are designed to enhance the affiliation between the prospect and the University.

Central Development Collaboration

Coordinates with the Division of University Advancement colleagues to identify prospects for planned giving, corporate and foundation giving in partnership with the Office of University Research, and principal giving in partnership with the Principal Gifts Team. Protects confidential information. Perform other related duties as required and assigned.

Qualifications

Master's degree and four years of experience in a related advancement field or a Bachelor's degree and six years of related experience. Higher education can substitute for experience at a comparable rate.

A valid Florida Driver's License or the ability to obtain upon hire.

University Information

One of the nation's elite research universities, Florida State University preserves, expands, and disseminates knowledge in the sciences, technology, arts, humanities, and professions, while embracing a philosophy of learning strongly rooted in the traditions of the liberal arts and critical thinking. Founded in 1851, Florida State University is the oldest continuous site of higher education in Florida. FSU is a community steeped in tradition that fosters research and encourages creativity. At FSU, there’s the excitement of being part of a vibrant academic and professional community, surrounded by people whose ideas are shaping tomorrow’s news!

Learn more about Florida State University and our campuses.

FSU Total Rewards

FSU offers a robust Total Rewards package. Visit our FSU Total Rewards website to learn more about our Compensation, Benefits, Wellness, Recognition, and Employee Development programs.

Use our FSU Total Compensation Calculator to calculate Total Compensation options based on potential salary, benefits and retirement contributions, earned leave, and other employment-related perks.

How To Apply

If qualified and interested in a specific job opening as advertised, apply through the FSU Careers site (jobs.fsu.edu). If you are a current FSU employee, apply through myFSU Self Service.

Applicants are required to complete the online application with all applicable information. Applications must include all work history up to ten years, and education details even if attaching a resume.

Considerations

This is an A&P position.

This position requires successful completion of a criminal history background check.

Must be willing to drive.

This position is being advertised as open until filled.

Equal Employment Opportunity

FSU is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer.

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Director of Operations

Pond Lehocky Giordano | Philadelphia, PA

In-Person (3–4 Days/Week)

Salary: $125,000–$150,000 + Bonus

Pond Lehocky Giordano is one of the largest workers' compensation and disability law firms in the country. Built in Philadelphia, the firm is now executing an ambitious national expansion strategy across multiple states, supported by significant investments in technology, operations, and organizational infrastructure.

We are seeking a highly analytical and execution-focused Director of Operations
to work directly alongside the CEO and COO in driving the firm's next phase of growth. This is a high-impact leadership role focused on translating strategy into action and building the operational systems that support a rapidly scaling organization.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Operations

Develop analyses, financial models, and executive-level recommendations that inform firm-wide decisions
Partner directly with senior leadership to drive strategic initiatives and organizational growth
Translate executive priorities into actionable project plans and measurable outcomes

Cross-Functional Leadership

Lead execution across operations, finance, marketing, and technology teams
Manage strategic projects from planning through implementation
Establish and maintain operational rhythms, metrics, and accountability structures

Technology & Process Improvement

Support the firm's legal technology ecosystem, including Litify, automation tools, AI-driven solutions, and intake platforms
Partner with vendors and internal stakeholders to improve adoption and maximize ROI on technology investments
Identify opportunities to drive efficiency and operational leverage through process improvement

Growth & Expansion

Support new market launches and expansion initiatives
Assist with workforce planning, organizational design, and infrastructure development
Build scalable systems and processes that support continued national growth

Qualifications

2–4 years of experience in management consulting (MBB or equivalent) or financial services
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to structure ambiguous business challenges
Experience developing financial models, business cases, and strategic recommendations
Ability to operate effectively in both strategic and highly detailed environments
Exceptional project management and execution skills
Strong communication skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with legal technology, litigation finance, or Salesforce-based organizations
Exposure to high-growth, operationally intensive environments
Proven ability to drive cross-functional initiatives and deliver results

What We Offer

Direct exposure to executive leadership and major strategic initiatives
Opportunity to help scale one of the nation's fastest-growing plaintiff-side law firms
Competitive base salary of $125,000–$150,000 plus performance-based bonus
High-visibility role with significant growth potential
Collaborative, high-performance culture focused on execution and results

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations (Home Care)
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
Compassionate Connections Home Care is a locally rooted, minority-owned home care organization providing personalized in-home support for seniors, veterans, and families across Florida. Services range from companionship and personal care to 24/7 private duty and veteran-directed care, combining the warmth of family with the reliability of a licensed healthcare provider. The company emphasizes consistency by carefully matching clients with caregivers who align with their needs and lifestyle. Caregivers are thoroughly vetted, trained, and supported to deliver high-quality, dignified care. Through innovative technology and partnerships with VA programs, rehab centers, and community organizations, Compassionate Connections Home Care keeps families connected and is expanding its care model through franchising.
Role Description
The Director of Operations (Home Care) is a full-time, on-site role based in Eustis, FL, responsible for overseeing daily operational activities across the agency. This role leads and supports office and field teams, ensuring consistent, high-quality client service and compliance with regulatory and company standards. Key responsibilities include managing staffing levels and scheduling, optimizing workflows, and monitoring performance metrics to drive efficiency and service excellence. The Director of Operations will develop and manage budgets, analyze operational data, and implement process improvements to support growth and franchising initiatives. This role also serves as a primary point of contact for key partners and families, addressing escalated concerns, supporting caregiver engagement, and reinforcing the organization’s mission and values in all operational decisions.
Qualifications

Strong Operations Management skills, including workflow optimization, process improvement, and compliance oversight.
Demonstrated Analytical Skills to interpret data, track performance metrics, and make informed operational decisions.
Proven Team Management capabilities, with experience leading multidisciplinary staff, coaching, and performance management.
Experience with Budgeting, financial planning, and resource allocation in a service-based or healthcare environment.
Excellent Customer Service skills, including conflict resolution, relationship building, and client satisfaction management.
Prior leadership experience in home care, healthcare, or related fields is strongly preferred.
Knowledge of state home care regulations, quality standards, and best practices for in-home support services.
Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving abilities, with comfort working in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in healthcare administration, business, or a related field; equivalent experience may be considered.
Ability to work on-site in Eustis, FL, with occasional travel within the service area as needed.

Please apply at: https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/e/1FAIpQLScaoWDFLyArlL6vLdq16-aN55d6sqWMJl5F-DgnibQ-0bXuhQ/viewform

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Company Background
The Pineapple Agency is a creative experiential agency focused on immersive brand activations and live performance stages, while The Juice Shop is a co-located fabrication shop that brings those concepts to life through physical production. Together, they represent a hybrid of project-based creative services and fabrication operations, requiring disciplined operational planning, production management, and reliable on-time, on-budget delivery.
Addition LLC is redefining luxury hospitality through innovative technology and experiences. The company currently focuses on pioneering technology solutions for elite hospitality brands while also building out a portfolio that now includes The Pineapple Agency and The Juice Shop.
Position Overview:
Addition LLC is seeking a Director of Operations to lead the operations function across Addition and its two subsidiaries: The Pineapple Agency, the creative business, and The Juice Shop, the fabrication business.
This leader will build and run a simple, scalable, and thorough operating system that supports a growing, project-based business and consistently delivers high-quality work on time and on budget.
The right candidate is a hands-on operations leader who can create structure, improve visibility, and establish disciplined processes without adding unnecessary complexity. This person will own the day-to-day delivery engine across two distinct but connected businesses, leading both the client-facing project and account management team and the shop floor production, facilities, inventory, and logistics functions that bring projects to life.
Industry background is important for this role. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in the same or a closely related industry, such as experiential marketing, live events, custom fabrication, scenic or set production, exhibits and environments, or specialty manufacturing, where creative concepts are produced and delivered as physical, project-based work.
Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the operations function, including project and account management, shop floor production, facilities, inventory, and shipping and logistics.Build simple, scalable, and thorough operating processes and systems that support current operations and future growth.Lead, manage, and develop the project and account management team, ensuring deliverables meet client and agency expectations, on budget and within deadlines, across multiple concurrent projects and clients.Establish consistent project management methodology, including detailed work plans, resource allocation, status reporting, risk and issue management, and clear hand-offs across departments.Oversee the shop floor and production staff at The Juice Shop, including scheduling, capacity planning, workflow, quality control, and safety on the fabrication floor.Direct facilities management for the company’s shop and office spaces, including maintenance, equipment uptime, vendor and service contracts, and a safe, compliant, well-run physical environment.Own inventory management, including materials planning, purchasing coordination, stock levels, tracking, and controls that reduce waste and protect project margins.Manage shipping and logistics for inbound materials and outbound deliverables, including freight, carriers, on-site delivery and installation timing, and reverse logistics for reusable assets.Partner closely with the Director of Finance on project costing, budgets, burn tracking, estimating inputs, and post-project review to protect and improve project profitability.Define project scopes, contribute to statement of work assumptions, create staff plans, and produce realistic timelines in coordination with creative, production, and account teams.Coordinate vendor negotiation and management, contracting outside resources and vendors as needed per project, and holding partners to scope, schedule, and quality standards.Develop clear operational reporting and dashboards covering project status, production capacity, on-time delivery, utilization, inventory, and operational KPIs.Establish and maintain operational policies, approval workflows, and standard operating procedures that improve accountability without slowing the business down.Drive continuous improvement across the delivery engine, identifying bottlenecks and implementing practical solutions that increase speed, quality, and capacity.Hire, manage, and develop operations, project management, and production team members over time as the business grows.Act as a strategic partner to the CEO and leadership team on capacity planning, organizational design, investment in equipment and systems, and operational risk management.
Candidate ProfileThe ideal candidate combines strong operational discipline with practical, hands-on judgment. This person knows how to build processes that are rigorous enough to support scale, while still being simple enough for teams to follow consistently across both a creative agency and a fabrication shop. A bold leader who is not afraid to hold the line on quality, timelines, and budgets.
Required QualificationsBachelor’s degree in Operations, Business, Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field, or equivalent experience.5 - 8 years of progressive operations experience, including meaningful leadership responsibility over both people and processes.Extensive experience in the same or a closely related industry, such as experiential marketing, live events, custom fabrication, scenic or set production, exhibits and environments, or specialty manufacturing.Proven experience leading project and account management teams in a fast-paced, client-facing, project-based environment.Direct experience overseeing shop floor or production operations, including scheduling, capacity planning, quality control, and safety.Working knowledge of facilities management, inventory control, and shipping and logistics.Strong understanding of project management methodology, tools, and best practices.Ability to work hands-on in a lean environment and personally drive execution.Strong organizational, planning, and multi-tasking skills to manage numerous programs and resources simultaneously.Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with creative, production, financial, and executive stakeholders.
Preferred QualificationsExperience in project-based businesses such as experiential marketing, live events, fabrication, scenic production, manufacturing, specialty services, or creative production.Experience operating across multiple entities or business lines under one organization.Experience implementing or upgrading project management, ERP, inventory, or production systems.Experience with budgeting, project costing, or margin management in partnership with finance.Experience supporting founder-led, entrepreneurial, or newly integrated businesses.
What Success Looks LikeWithin the first 12 months, the Director of Operations will be expected to:Build a clear and reliable operating cadence across project management and the shop floor.Improve on-time, on-budget delivery and overall visibility into project status and production capacity.Establish standardized operational processes and reporting across Addition, The Pineapple Agency, and The Juice Shop.Tighten inventory, facilities, and shipping and logistics processes to reduce waste and improve reliability.Implement practical controls and workflows that improve accountability without slowing the business down.Provide leadership with timely, decision-useful operational insights.
Leadership CharacteristicsProcess-oriented without being bureaucratic.Highly organized, detail-oriented, and dependable.Commercially minded and comfortable partnering with creative leaders and finance.Calm under pressure and able to prioritize in a fast-moving environment.Curious, resourceful, and committed to continuous improvement.Willing to roll up sleeves and solve problems directly, on the floor and with the team.
Candidate PitchThis is an opportunity to build the operational foundation for a distinctive, multi-entity business that sits at the intersection of creativity, production, and execution. The Director of Operations will have broad responsibility, direct exposure to leadership, and the chance to create real operating leverage by putting strong delivery processes in place early. There will be opportunities for significant upside potential. For candidates who enjoy bringing order, clarity, and operational discipline to entrepreneurial businesses, this role offers meaningful scope and impact.

new_jobs.title: Executive Director -  Assisted Living
new_jobs.description:
Position Summary

Responsible for day-to-day operations of the community. Assures compliance with all laws, rules, regulations, policies, and procedures. Oversight of property management, Employee Partner administration, and provision of Resident Services. Promotes the company’s mission and philosophy.

REPORTS TO:
REGIONAL DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS

Position Responsibilities

Resident Services
Assures implementation of policies and procedures relating to Resident care.
Supervises and assists in provision of all Resident services, including the coordination of those provided by independently contracted ancillary health care providers as requested by Residents and families.
Conducts assessments, negotiates service plans, develops service schedules, and updates service information as indicates by Resident need.
Ensures that Residents are appropriately assessed on a timely basis and charged in accordance with that assessment.
Assists Residents to participate in self-governance activity through Resident council and committee meetings.
Acts as an advocate for Residents and maintains communication with designated agents as requested and required.
Assures quality of Resident services.
Assures a high degree of customer satisfaction at residence. Promptly investigates complaints and reports findings and appropriate recommendation to the President of SSL Management Company. Ensures follow-up communication occurs to complainant and that substantiated complaints are resolved to reasonable satisfaction of complainant.
Ensures compliance with all rules and regulations related to Resident care (i.e., HIPAA, State Regulations and OSHA, etc.).
Maintains a professional demeanor with all Residents and their loved ones.

Employee Partner Administration
Recruits, interviews, hires, supervises, evaluates, and disciplines community Employee Partners.
Assures the availability of qualified, appropriately trained community Employee Partners.
Supervises and assists in orientation and training of community Employee Partners.
Coordinates and assists in administration of wages/benefits for community Employee Partners, assures accuracy of Employee Partner records and approves payment for work performed.
Assures Employee Partners’ adherence to all policies and procedures, all work, safety, and administrative rules.
Ensures compliance with all rules and regulations related to Employee Partners (i.e., OSHA, Wage & Hours, FMLA, etc.).
Promotes the company’s mission and philosophy.
Maintains a professional demeanor with all Employee Partners.

Sales/Marketing
Develops and implements sales/marketing plans with President of SSL Management Company and Sales Team to accomplish occupancy targets.
Develops and maintains a favorable public image; participates in community organizations for the promotion of the community and SSL.
Provides external marketing efforts on a weekly basis.
Meets sales/marketing goals or adjusts sales plans accordingly to accomplish occupancy targets.

Property Management
Coordinates community, Resident units, common areas, and adjacent grounds with Maintenance Director.

Financial Responsibility
Operates with resources provided, assures income and expenses are controlled using annual budget projections.
Assures all financial records are properly maintained.
Participates in the development of an annual budget.
Assures that Resident invoices are delivered, and the residence’s accounts receivables are collected on a timely, consistent basis.

Administrative Responsibility
Administrates, coordinates, and directs all activities in accordance with policies and procedures.
Assures safety and security of community’s contents and inhabitants (Residents, Employee Partners, and visitors).
Assures proper use of equipment/supplies.
Maintains and provides all data as requested, required.
Will be on call; arranges appropriate coverage when absent from community or unavailable to respond to emergencies.
Performs other duties as assigned or required.
Assures that adverse findings by company Quality reviews or state surveyors are promptly corrected and remain in compliance.

Employment Requirements

Effective supervisory skills.
Ability to communicate and work with all levels of the company’s Employee Partners effectively.
Ability to recognize and communicate problems appropriately.
Ability to relate to the public, Residents, families, Employee Partners, and other professionals appropriately.
Ability to maintain accurate records and provide information as requested and required.
Ability to communicate proficiently in English, verbally and in writing.
Ability to work flexible hours to meet requirements of the job and be on call.
Ability to lift up to 50 pounds, 30 pounds regularly.
Maintains car in good working order, current driver’s license in good standing and auto insurance meeting company minimums is current.
CPR certified.
Ability to teach/train others effectively.

Training And Experience

Experience in managing a program or community for older adults preferred. One year of supervisory experience required. Knowledge of an interest in working with older adults required. Holds a current CPR Certificate or can obtain a CPR Certificate within 90 days of being hired.

Education

High school diploma required. College credits or degree preferred or sufficient education to satisfy state experience requirement. Maintain CPR/First Aid Certification. Attendance at mandatory in-service training. Meet continuing education requirements on job classification and position.

Supervises and assists in provision of all Resident services, including the coordination of those provided by independently contracted ancillary health care providers as requested by Residents and families.
Conducts assessments, negotiates service plans, develops service schedules, and updates service information as indicates by Resident need.
Ensures that Residents are appropriately assessed on a timely basis and charged in accordance with that assessment.
Assists Residents to participate in self-governance activity through Resident council and committee meetings.
Acts as an advocate for Residents and maintains communication with designated agents as requested and required.
Assures quality of Resident services.
Assures a high degree of customer satisfaction at residence. Promptly investigates complaints and reports findings and appropriate recommendation to the President of SSL Management Company. Ensures follow-up communication occurs to complainant and that substantiated complaints are resolved to reasonable satisfaction of complainant.
Ensures compliance with all rules and regulations related to Resident care (i.e., HIPAA, State Regulations and OSHA, etc.).
Maintains a professional demeanor with all Residents and their loved ones.

Employee Partner Administration
Recruits, interviews, hires, supervises, evaluates, and disciplines community Employee Partners.
Assures the availability of qualified, appropriately trained community Employee Partners.
Supervises and assists in orientation and training of community Employee Partners.
Coordinates and assists in administration of wages/benefits for community Employee Partners, assures accuracy of Employee Partner records and approves payment for work performed.
Assures Employee Partners’ adherence to all policies and procedures, all work, safety, and administrative rules.
Ensures compliance with all rules and regulations related to Employee Partners (i.e., OSHA, Wage & Hours, FMLA, etc.).
Promotes the company’s mission and philosophy.
Maintains a professional demeanor with all Employee Partners.

Sales/Marketing
Develops and implements sales/marketing plans with President of SSL Management Company and Sales Team to accomplish occupancy targets.
Develops and maintains a favorable public image; participates in community organizations for the promotion of the community and SSL.
Provides external marketing efforts on a weekly basis.
Meets sales/marketing goals or adjusts sales plans accordingly to accomplish occupancy targets.

Property Management
Coordinates community, Resident units, common areas, and adjacent grounds with Maintenance Director.

Financial Responsibility
Operates with resources provided, assures income and expenses are controlled using annual budget projections.
Assures all financial records are properly maintained.
Participates in the development of an annual budget.
Assures that Resident invoices are delivered, and the residence’s accounts receivables are collected on a timely, consistent basis.

Administrative Responsibility
Administrates, coordinates, and directs all activities in accordance with policies and procedures.
Assures safety and security of community’s contents and inhabitants (Residents, Employee Partners, and visitors).
Assures proper use of equipment/supplies.
Maintains and provides all data as requested, required.
Will be on call; arranges appropriate coverage when absent from community or unavailable to respond to emergencies.
Performs other duties as assigned or required.
Assures that adverse findings by company Quality reviews or state surveyors are promptly corrected and remain in compliance.

Employment Requirements

Effective supervisory skills.
Ability to communicate and work with all levels of the company’s Employee Partners effectively.
Ability to recognize and communicate problems appropriately.
Ability to relate to the public, Residents, families, Employee Partners, and other professionals appropriately.
Ability to maintain accurate records and provide information as requested and required.
Ability to communicate proficiently in English, verbally and in writing.
Ability to work flexible hours to meet requirements of the job and be on call.
Ability to lift up to 50 pounds, 30 pounds regularly.
Maintains car in good working order, current driver’s license in good standing and auto insurance meeting company minimums is current.
CPR certified.
Ability to teach/train others effectively.

Training And Experience

Experience in managing a program or community for older adults preferred. One year of supervisory experience required. Knowledge of an interest in working with older adults required. Holds a current CPR Certificate or can obtain a CPR Certificate within 90 days of being hired.

Education

High school diploma required. College credits or degree preferred or sufficient education to satisfy state experience requirement. Maintain CPR/First Aid Certification. Attendance at mandatory in-service training. Meet continuing education requirements on job classification and position.

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
Temple University's Fischer-Shain Center for Financial Services at the Fox School of Business is searching for an Executive Director (PT)!

Lead Industry Partnerships. Inspire Future Banking Leaders. Expand the Impact of Financial Services Education. Shape the Future of Banking Leadership.

Position Summary

The Fox School of Business at Temple University is seeking an accomplished financial services executive to serve as the Executive Director of the Fischer-Shain Center for Financial Services. This unique part-time leadership opportunity is ideal for an established banking or financial services professional who is passionate about developing future industry leaders while leveraging a distinguished professional network to strengthen connections between academia and practice. The Executive Director will play a pivotal role in expanding industry partnerships, engaging senior executives and advisory board members, cultivating philanthropic support, and creating transformative experiences that prepare students for successful careers in commercial banking and financial services. If you are a strategic relationship builder who is energized by mentorship, leadership, and advancing the future of the profession, we invite you to join one of the nation's leading business schools and make a lasting impact.

The Fischer-Shain Center for Financial Services serves as a gateway for students to careers in the financial services industry through programming such as: case study competitions, practice-oriented conferences, research grant sponsorship, industry guest speakers, scholarships, and other professional development opportunities. The Executive Director will lead these efforts, with a focus on cultivating external partnerships, and will collaborate frequently with Fox leadership, the Center’s Advisory Board, industry professionals, and prospective donors.

Perform other duties as assigned.

Hourly Rate: $41.00-$46.00/hr.

Required Education and Experience

* Bachelor’s degree
* At least 7 years of experience in the financial services industry
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

Preferred Education and Experience

* Commercial banking experience
* Master’s degree

Required Skills and Abilities

* Strong knowledge of the commercial banking industry
* Exceptional organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines
* Demonstrated verbal, written, and interpersonal skills with the ability to form effective working relationships with diverse groups of industry professionals, faculty, staff, students, and prospective donors * Skill in envisioning, designing, planning, executing, and leading industry-focused events, both virtual and in-person
* Ability to monitor and manage budgets effectively
* Technical proficiency in Microsoft Office, Zoom, and other standard software applications
* Demonstrated ability or potential to connect students to the commercial banking industry in ways that build professional confidence, readiness, and long-term career awareness
* Ability to cultivate and expand a professional network, with the ability to develop new industry and individual relationships
* Skill in engaging and communicating with an advisory board, including the ability to present program progress, articulate a vision for center growth, and advance a culture of philanthropic investment in the center's mission

Preferred Skills and Abilities

* Ability to teach one or more undergraduate classes in areas related to commercial banking

Essential Duties

* Manages the Center’s Advisory Board, organizes meetings, recruits board members, solicits curriculum feedback from board members, collaborates to identify networking, internship, and employment opportunities in the financial services industry, with a focus on banking
* Collaborates with Fox Development Office staff to identify and cultivate philanthropic gifts and oversee scholarship application and award process
* Oversees event programming, develops topics and formats, recruits potential partners, panelists, and industry guests
* Prepares and guides students for effective participation in co- and extracurricular activities focused on the banking industry
* Contributes collaboratively with department and school faculty, and school staff, to prepare students for career success

This position is assigned a hybrid work arrangement (on-campus and remote), the duration of this hybrid work arrangement is at the discretion of Temple University and the Department.

new_jobs.title: Senior Executive Director, Development Nashville
new_jobs.description:
At the American Cancer Society, we're working to end cancer as we know it, for everyone. Our employees and 1.3 million volunteers are raising the bar every single day. We are a culture comprised of diverse backgrounds and experience, to better serve our communities.

The people who work at the American Cancer Society focus their diverse talents on our lifesaving mission. It is a calling. And the people who answer it are fulfilled.

The Senior Executive Director will be responsible for leading the Society’s fundraising activities for a market-based area, including management of a team of fundraising professionals. This role is responsible for the achievement of aggressive revenue targets through leveraging external relationships for corporate engagement, major gifts and fundraising events. This role is accountable for the engagement of executive leadership volunteers (Area Board, CEOs Against Cancer Chapters, event leadership teams) and driving integration with our mission pillars of Discovery, Patient Support & Advocacy.

Accountable for an area that includes:

1 large MSA, with limited activity in surrounding geography and secondary markets
F1000 headquarters & large health systems
A pipeline of individual wealth
Corporate relations & philanthropy staff
$500K+ event(s)
$5M - $8M revenue accountability
This is a 100% remote role; however the ideal candidate will reside within the Nashville, Tennessee area.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

Drives aggressive revenue growth expectations through multi-faceted, multi-year corporate partnerships, individual and foundation major gifts, and mega events ($500K+). Leads with a growth mindset, setting and achieving ambitions year-over-year revenue goals. 20%
Leads donor-centric revenue generation directly, and through leadership of a team of fundraising professionals, leveraging volunteer engagement and relationship management. 20%
Serves as a lead relationship manager, “elevator”, and “closer” for top tier accounts and partnerships. Identifies and secures large-scale opportunities, guiding prospects from initial engagement through commitment and execution. 20%
Recruits and manages influential Area Board(s) and/or a CEOs Against Cancer Chapter comprised of executive leaders and community influencers; ensures executive engagement in event chair roles. 15%
Builds and maintains significant C-suite relationships to secure transformational gifts. 15%
Drives integration with our mission pillars of Discovery, Patient Support & Advocacy. 10%

EXPERIENCE/QUALIFICATIONS:

Minimum Degree Required: Bachelor’s Degree or Equivalent
Preferred Degree: Master’s degree a plus
Years of experience: 6+ years fundraising & team leadership, sales management or equivalent experience with multi-million-dollar targets

KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITY:

Proven ability to design and execute comprehensive fundraising strategies that drive significant revenue growth across multiple channels.
Expertise in building and maintaining long-term relationships with major donors, foundations, and corporate partners, with a focus on donor engagement and retention.
Demonstrated success in leading, mentoring, and motivating high-performing development teams to achieve ambitious fundraising goals.
Proficient in using donor data and analytics to inform strategy, track performance, and optimize fundraising efforts.
Skilled in leveraging board members and volunteers as fundraising ambassadors and partners in donor outreach.
Ability to represent the organization as a spokesperson, including through media channels
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft compelling cases for support and deliver persuasive presentations to diverse audiences.
Ability to identify, cultivate, and secure strategic partnerships with corporations, foundations, and community organizations.
Experience leading high-impact fundraising events, from concept through execution, with a focus on chair recruitment, return on investment and donor experience.
Strong financial acumen to manage fundraising budgets, forecast revenue, and ensure cost-effective operations.
Creative thinker with the ability to adapt fundraising strategies in response to changing donor behaviors and market conditions.
Monitors metrics and milestones to chart progress against expectations; course corrects as needed to ensure goal achievement
Ability to navigate an organization’s culture and help others to do the same
Demonstrated expertise in building strong relationships with team members through effective feedback delivered with emotional intelligence

TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS:

Limited overnight travel to area, region & enterprise meetings and events

PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:

No specific physical requirements

The starting rate is $115,000 to $150,000. The final candidate's relevant experience/skills will be considered before an offer is extended. Actual starting pay will vary based on non-discriminatory factors including, but not limited to, geographic location, experience, skills, specialty, and education.

ACS provides staff a generous paid time off policy; medical, dental, retirement benefits, wellness programs, and professional development programs to enhance staff skills. Further details on our benefits can be found on our careers site at: jobs.cancer.org/benefits. We are a proud equal opportunity employer.
